worked, played (acrescenta -ed)
went, ate, saw (mudam a forma)
I didn’t see him. (did not)
Did you call her?
I am leaving tomorrow.
She is going to study medicine.
bigger than, more interesting than
the biggest, the most expensive
some / any / no (ex.: some water, any problems, no idea)
How much sugar? / How many apples?
somebody, anything, nothing
at 5pm / on Monday / in June
quickly, happily (terminam em -ly)
You should see a doctor.
You must stop. / You mustn’t smoke here.
I would like a coffee, please.
I was tired because I worked late; so I slept; then I woke up.
I work daily (rotina) vs I am working now (agora)

Passado simples de to be (was / were): Estrutura básica de perguntas com How
How old are you? / How often do you exercise?
I have seen that movie. (experiência/vida – momento não especificado)

Passado simples de to be (was / were): Present perfect com just, already, yet
I've just eaten / I've already finished / I haven't called yet
I have lost my key (ainda está perdida) vs I lost it yesterday (ação concluída no passado específico)
for three years (duração) / since 2020 (ponto de início)
I was sleeping when the phone rang.
I was watching TV (ação em andamento) when she arrived (ação curta que interrompe)
It will rain tomorrow. / I will answer the phone.
If it rains, I will stay home. (possível/real)
If I were rich, I would travel the world. (hipotético/improvável)
as big as a house (tão grande quanto)
myself, yourself, himself (ex.: I did it myself)
English is spoken here.
The car was repaired yesterday.
Swimming is good for your health.
I enjoy playing, finish working, avoid eating junk food.
I want to go, decide to leave, hope to see you.
She made me laugh (forçar) / She let me go (permitir) / She allowed me to enter (permitir formal)
So do I (positivo) / Neither do I (negativo) / I love it too / I don't like it either
I used to play piano when I was a child (mas agora não jogo mais)
You like coffee, don't you? / She isn't here, is she?
I have been waiting for an hour. (ênfase na duração da ação inacabada)
I had eaten before they arrived. (ação anterior a outra ação passada)
I will be working at 8pm. (ação em andamento num momento futuro específico)
I will have finished by then. (ação concluída antes de um momento futuro)
If I had known, I would have come. (situação impossível no passado)
If she had studied, she would be here now. (condição passada + resultado presente)
The house is being painted (presente contínuo passivo). Ex.: was painted / has been painted / will be painted
He said that he was tired. (mudança de tempo verbal e pronomes)
She asked where I lived. (inversão desaparece, sem "do/does/did")
He told me to sit down. (verbo + objeto + infinitivo)
stop to smoke (para para fumar) vs stop smoking (para de fumar)
She must be at home (certeza) / can't be (impossibilidade) / might be (possibilidade)
She must have been late / He could have done better (dedução sobre passado)
The man who called is my uncle. (sem vírgulas; essencial para identificar)
My car, which is red, is fast. (com vírgulas; informação adicional)
Never have I seen such beauty. (ênfase negativa – verbo antes do sujeito)
quite good / rather difficult / pretty nice / fairly common (intensidade média)
I do like it! / She does speak English / He did call me (contrariando uma negação ou dúvida)
Although tired, she worked / Despite the rain / In spite of that / Whereas I like coffee, she likes tea
interested in / afraid of / proud of / good at / responsible for
I had been waiting for hours before she arrived. (ênfase na duração de uma ação contínua anterior a outra ação no passado)
I will have been working here for 5 years by next month. (ênfase na duração contínua até um ponto futuro)
Had I known about the party, I would have come. (equivalente a "If I had known")
What I need is a break. / It was John who called. (ênfase em um elemento da frase)
I suggest that he go / It's important that she be on time. (forma base do verbo, sem -s)
"I love coffee." – "So do I." / "I've never been there." – "Neither have I." (omissão/substituição de verbos)
Having been invited to the party, I decided to go. (ação passiva anterior à principal)
To be loved is more important than to be rich. / She hopes to be chosen.
The book (that/which) I read was great. (sujeito? não – objeto: pode omitir)
Direct: "I am tired." → Indirect: He said he was tired. ("backshift" obrigatório)
Not only did she arrive late, but she also forgot her keys. (inversão após "not only")
So beautiful was the view that we stopped immediately. / Such was his anger that he left.
"I might come" → He said he might come. (alguns modais não mudam: could, would, should, might, ought to)
Nevertheless (apesar disso), Consequently (portanto), Furthermore (além disso)
The government should help the rich and the poor. (os ricos / os pobres – grupo plural)
The more you study, the less you realize you know. (correlação proporcional)
If possible, call me. / When necessary, take a break. (conectivo + adjetivo/participio)
I had my hair cut / I got the car fixed. (alguém fez por mim)
I wish I would have known earlier. (arrependimento sobre passado – informal, mas comum)

Passado simples de to be (was / were): It's time / I'd rather + passado subjuntivo
It's time you went to bed. / I'd rather you didn't smoke here. (passado simples com sentido presente/futuro)
So powerful was the storm that many trees fell. (inversão após "so + adjetivo")
He has and always will love her. (has loved / will love – auxiliar mantido, verbo omitido)
I was wondering if you can help me. (passado educado + presente real)
A few more minutes, and we would have missed the train. (= If we had arrived a few minutes later)
This book I really love (ênfase: objeto no início)
The cancellation of the meeting surprised everyone. (verbo → substantivo)
He said he would leave, and did so (substitui "left")
I would have thought you'd told him (expectativa não confirmada, tom educado)
What more could one ask? (inversão sujeito-auxiliar em pergunta retórica)
He might have been going to call her. (cadeia modal: might + have + been + going to)
Be that as it may, we need to decide. (concessivo formal)

Passado simples de to be (was / were): Word order for focus and emphasis
Down came the rain (inversão advérbio de direção + verbo + sujeito)
The car is expected to be sold tomorrow. (dois verbos passivos: is expected + to be sold)
It was time we had left (passado perfeito subjuntivo em "It was time")
Sounds good (omissão de "That") / Coming? (omissão de "Are you")
This man, I believe, is honest. (oração intercalada sem conectivo)
To err is human (infinitivo como sujeito) / Swimming is fun (gerúndio)
Even if I had known, I wouldn't have cared. (concessão + condicional irreal)
In conclusion, what I've argued is that climate change is urgent. (organização explícita do discurso)
This I deny (objeto no início para ênfase formal)
